text: Which of the following describes the difference between 'Scaling Up' and 'Scaling Out' a Virtual Warehouse? (Choose two.)

options.a: Scaling up increases the T-shirt size to improve the performance of complex queries.
options.b: Scaling out adds more clusters to a warehouse to handle higher concurrency/more users.
options.c: Scaling up adds more storage space to the cloud services layer.
options.d: Scaling out moves the data to a faster storage provider.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to share a 'Database' that was created from a 'Share' with a third Snowflake account.
What is the primary function of 'Micro-partitions' in the Snowflake storage layer?

options.a: To provide a user-definable partitioning scheme.
options.b: To enable automatic data pruning during query execution based on metadata.
options.c: To store data in a row-based format for faster inserts.
options.d: To encrypt data at the column level.

text: Which role is recommended for managing all security-related objects like network policies and MFA?
What is the maximum retention period for 'Fail-safe' in a Snowflake account?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses its own serverless compute resources and does not consume credits from your virtual warehouses.
Which of the following can be a source for an 'External Stage'? (Choose three.)
What happens if a warehouse has 'Auto-suspend' enabled and reaches the specified timeout period with no queries running?

options.a: It stays active but stops billing.
options.b: It is automatically suspended and stops consuming credits.
options.c: It resizes to X-Small to save credits.
options.d: It moves to a standby mode.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Pipes' in a specific database/schema?
True or False: Data shared via 'Direct Sharing' is only available between accounts in the same cloud region.
What is the primary benefit of 'Time Travel'?

options.a: To speed up historical analytical queries.
options.b: To restore data from accidental drops or modifications within a specific retention window.
options.c: To archive data to low-cost storage automatically.
options.d: To move data between cloud providers.

text: Which Snowflake component provides the infrastructure to manage metadata, security, and query compilation?
What is the default T-shirt size for a Virtual Warehouse when created via SQL if not specified?
True or False: Snowflake handles the encryption of data 'at rest' using AES-256 automatically.
Which command is used to upload a local file into a Snowflake internal stage?
What does 'Zero-copy cloning' mean in Snowflake?

options.b: The data is physically duplicated to a new storage bucket.
options.c: New metadata points to the existing data micro-partitions without immediate duplication of the data files.
options.d: Cloning only works for empty tables.

text: Which view is used to monitor the credit usage of a specific virtual warehouse?
True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual User level to limit their credit spend.
What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.a: To speed up the loading of individual rows.
options.b: To restrict which rows a user can see in a table based on their role or other attributes.
options.c: To manage how many rows are stored in a micro-partition.
options.d: To encrypt specific rows of data.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the minimum requirement for 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?
True or False: Data in a 'Temporary' table is automatically deleted when the user session ends.
Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service'?

options.a: It creates a persistent data structure to speed up point lookup queries on large tables.
options.b: It is a free tool in the Standard edition.
options.c: It is used to search for files in the internal stage.
options.d: It replaces the need for clustering keys entirely.

text: What is the benefit of the 'Snowflake Marketplace'?

options.a: Users can download Snowflake software versions.
options.b: It allows organizations to discover and access third-party data and services without data movement.
options.c: It is a hardware store for cloud servers.
options.d: It is where users buy Snowflake credits.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Users' in the Snowflake account?
True or False: You can set a 'Network Policy' at the individual User level to override the account-level policy.
What does 'Pruning' refer to in Snowflake query execution?

options.a: Deleting old data to save space.
options.b: The process where Snowflake skips reading micro-partitions that it knows do not contain relevant data for the query.
options.d: Removing metadata from the cloud services layer.

text: Which role is required to manage 'Organizational' tasks like creating and managing multiple Snowflake accounts?
True or False: Snowflake handles the automatic 'Rotation of encryption keys' for its customers.
Which Snowflake component provides the results for a query if it was recently run and the data has not changed?
What happens to the credit consumption when a virtual warehouse is in a 'Suspended' state?

options.a: It continues at a lower rate.
options.c: It is charged a flat monthly fee.

text: True or False: A 'Transient' table survives a system failure and is persisted across sessions.
Which command is used to download data from a Snowflake stage to a local machine?
What is the primary benefit of 'External Tables'?

options.a: They are faster than standard tables.
options.b: They allow you to query data sitting in cloud storage without loading it into Snowflake.
options.c: They do not require a warehouse.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the first to include 'Column-level Security' like Dynamic Data Masking?
True or False: You can apply a 'Masking Policy' to a column in a Snowflake 'View'.
What does the 'ON_ERROR = CONTINUE' parameter do in a COPY INTO command?

options.a: It stops the load at the first error.
options.b: It skips files with errors and continues loading valid data from other files.

text: Which role is primarily used for creating 'Warehouses' and 'Databases' in the Snowflake account?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Multi-factor Authentication' (MFA) for the classic web interface.
What is the purpose of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: To access and recover data that has been modified or deleted within a specified time window.
options.c: To archive data to long-term storage.

text: Which Snowflake component handles the coordination of 'Transaction Management'?
What happens if a multi-cluster warehouse is set to 'Auto-scale' and high query load occurs?

options.a: It increases the T-shirt size of the cluster.
options.b: It automatically starts additional clusters of the same size to handle the load.
options.c: It moves queries to the cloud services layer.
options.d: It notifies the administrator to manually add clusters.

text: True or False: Data shared via a 'Share' object is read-only for the consumer account.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Stages' in a specific database/schema?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention for a 'Permanent' table in the Standard edition?
True or False: Snowflake 'Stored Procedures' can be written in Javascript.
Which of the following describes the behavior of the 'Economy' scaling policy for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.a: It starts clusters immediately to prevent queuing.
options.b: It only starts a new cluster if it estimates there is enough load to keep it busy for at least 6 minutes.
options.c: It limits the warehouse to X-Small size.
options.d: It only uses one cluster regardless of load.

text: True or False: You can apply a 'Network Policy' at the individual user level in Snowflake.
What is the primary function of 'Resource Monitors'?

options.b: To limit and monitor the consumption of credits by virtual warehouses.
options.c: To track the size of the database storage.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'PrivateLink' and higher security compliance like HIPAA?
True or False: Data shared via 'Private Sharing' is encrypted in transit.
Which Snowflake component handles 'Query Parsing' and 'Optimization'?
What happens if a warehouse size is changed (e.g., Small to Medium) while queries are running?

options.b: The existing queries continue on the original resources, and new queries use the new resources.
options.d: All queries are moved to the new resources immediately.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Object Tagging' for tracking costs and sensitive data.
What is the function of a 'Stream' in Snowflake?

options.a: To load data continuously into a stage.
options.b: To track Change Data Capture (CDC) information for a table.
options.c: To connect to a streaming service like Kafka.
options.d: To process real-time analytics on query history.

text: Which Snowflake interface is a command-line tool for executing SQL queries and managing the account?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute and does not require a customer-managed warehouse.
What is the primary benefit of 'Clustering' a large table in Snowflake?

options.b: It improves query performance by enabling better data pruning.
options.c: It allows more users to connect simultaneously.
options.d: It encrypts the data at the cluster level.

text: Which role is the most privileged in a Snowflake account?
True or False: A 'Permanent' table has a 7-day Fail-safe period in all Snowflake editions.
Which Snowflake component provides the results for a query if it was run within the last 24 hours and the data is unchanged?
What happens if a Virtual Warehouse reaches its credit limit set by a Resource Monitor?

options.a: It is automatically resized to X-Small.
options.b: It can be configured to notify or suspend the warehouse immediately or after current queries finish.
options.d: The billing moves to an on-demand model.

text: True or False: Snowflake 'External Tables' can be used to query data in an Amazon S3 bucket.
What is the purpose of 'Dynamic Data Masking'?

options.a: To encrypt the data on disk.
options.b: To obscuring sensitive data in query results based on the user's role without changing the underlying data.
options.c: To compress data for faster searches.

text: Which Snowflake component handles 'Transaction Management' and 'Metadata'?
True or False: Snowflake handles the automatic 'Encryption of data at rest' for its users.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Resource Monitors'?
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.a: Deleting old data to save space.
options.b: Skipping micro-partitions that don't contain relevant data based on metadata and query filters.
options.c: Reducing the size of a warehouse.

text: True or False: A 'Transient' table has a Fail-safe period of 1 day.
Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Tri-Secret Secure' (customer-managed encryption keys)?
True or False: A 'Permanent' table can be undropped even after it has reached the end of its Time Travel period.
Which command is used to see the 'DDL' for an existing Snowflake object?
What happens if you run a 'CREATE OR REPLACE' command on a table that already exists?

options.a: It updates the table structure and keeps the data.
options.b: It drops the existing table and creates a new empty one.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'External Functions' to call an API hosted on Google Cloud Functions.
Which role is primarily responsible for creating and managing 'Users' and 'Roles' in Snowflake?
What is the result of 'Scaling Up' a Virtual Warehouse?

options.b: Increasing the T-shirt size (e.g., Small to Medium).

text: Which of the following describes the difference between 'Standard' and 'Economy' scaling policies for multi-cluster warehouses? (Choose two.)

options.a: Standard favors performance by starting clusters immediately.
options.b: Economy favors credit saving by only starting clusters if enough load is estimated.
options.c: Standard limits the size of the warehouse.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to share a 'Secure View' that joins data from multiple databases.
What is the primary function of 'Micro-partitions'?

options.b: To store data in an optimized, compressed, columnar format that enables automatic pruning.

text: Which role is recommended for daily administrative tasks like managing schemas and loading data?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention for a 'Permanent' table in the Business Critical edition?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute and does not require a customer-managed warehouse for ingestion.
Which of the following can be used as a source for an 'External Stage'? (Choose three.)
What happens if 'Auto-suspend' is enabled and a warehouse has been idle for the specified period?

options.a: It stops billing but stays active.
options.b: It is automatically suspended and stops consuming credits.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Pipes'?
True or False: Data shared via 'Direct Sharing' is only available within the same cloud region.
What is the primary benefit of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: Restoring data modified or deleted within the retention period.

text: Which Snowflake component provides metadata management and security coordination?
What is the default T-shirt size for a warehouse if not specified?
True or False: Snowflake handles the automatic 'Encryption of data at rest' using AES-256.
Which command is used to upload a local file to a Snowflake stage?
What does 'Zero-copy cloning' mean in Snowflake?

options.c: The metadata points to existing data files without duplication.

text: Which view monitors credit usage of a specific warehouse?
True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual User level.
What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.b: To restrict row visibility based on user role.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?
True or False: 'Temporary' tables are automatically deleted when the session ends.
Which describes the 'Search Optimization Service'?

options.a: Speeds up point lookup queries on large tables.

text: What is the benefit of the 'Snowflake Marketplace'?

options.b: Discovery and access of third-party data without movement.

text: Which command sees the list of 'Users'?
True or False: Network policies can be applied to individual users.
